channel.title: Posts on Cyberforks
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Cyberforks

item.title: I Automated My Freelance Bookkeeping With an AI and a Spreadsheet
item.description: How a simple command in Discord now handles receipt filing, PDF uploads, and spreadsheet entries automatically.

item.title: The Dual-Channel Auth Token Pattern for AI Actions
item.description: A security pattern for authorizing sensitive AI actions: generate a token, post it to one channel, confirm in another. Simple, auditable, and hard to spoof.

item.title: Why I Run a Personal AI Agent on My Own Machine
item.description: OpenClaw, self-hosted LLM routing, Discord as a command surface, and why this beats every chatbot app I've tried.

item.title: Automating Instagram Without the Instagram API
item.description: Meta's API is a nightmare of Facebook Pages and OAuth scopes. So we automated it with a browser instead.

item.title: Building a Pentest TTP Vault with Obsidian and an AI Scribe
item.description: A living knowledge base of techniques, tools, and attack chains — with a hard public/private separation and an AI scribe to reduce the friction.

item.title: cyber for breakfast. shitpost for dinner.
item.description:
Every security practitioner eventually gets tired of the noise — the endless stream of CVE notifications, patch Tuesdays, and vendor webinars that promise to solve the problem of humans being humans.

So we built something.

This blog is where Cyberforks documents what it actually looks like to automate the boring parts, build AI-assisted workflows for real security work, and occasionally meme about the absurdity of it all.

Some posts will be technical. Some will be sarcastic. All of them will be real — pulled from actual work, actual incidents, actual moments of “why does this not exist yet?”

item.title: I Gave My AI Assistant a Security Clearance System (And Audit Logs)
item.description: Building Tier 1/2/3 confirmation gates, auth tokens, and dual-channel security into an AI assistant workflow.

item.title: What Happens When You Make an AI the Dungeon Master
item.description: Running Curse of Strahd with an AI DM — how it works, where it breaks, and why it's surprisingly good at dramatic tension.
